danger_signals.meaning: Urgency and threat of failure; world-state pressure on player
Danger manifests through complex timing demands rather than direct enemy threat

safety_signals.meaning: Playful tone reduces threat perception; world designed for younger audience safety expectations
Audio cue establishing safe, entertainment-focused world-state

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stiff controls intentionally designed to create puzzle-solving difficulty rather than action challenge
Player forced into rapid decision-making; puzzle solving becomes stressful mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard platformer level layout and progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Audio (circus music) and visual style (comical animations) establish playful carnival world setting
Game based on Marsupilami character from André Franquin's comic franchise creates pre-established world context

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Perky, comical animations that telegraph movement and action states

notes: Source material provides minimal detail on visual communication systems. Wikipedia article focuses on reception and metadata rather than gameplay mechanics, visual design, or world-state manifestation systems. Game positioned toward younger audience with stiff controls and puzzle-solving emphasis over action. Tone established through 'perky comical animations' and 'plucky happy circus music' but specific visual danger/safety signals not documented in available source.
